title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Hangover
intro: Herbal teas for hangover are a type of herbal remedy that uses various plant extracts to alleviate the symptoms of a hangover, such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. These teas can treat hangover by providing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe the body and mind. Examples of herbal teas used to treat hangover include Peppermint tea, which eases nausea and digestive issues; Ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and aids digestion; Dandelion root tea, which helps to flush out toxins and rehydrate the body; Chamomile tea, which calms the mind and promotes relaxation; Lavender tea, which reduces stress and anxiety; and Green tea, which contains antioxidants that help to combat hangover symptoms. By consuming these herbal teas, individuals can alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with hangovers, promoting a faster recovery and a return to normal activities.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas have been traditionally used to alleviate hangover symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger, including gingerols and shogaols, help to combat nausea, dizziness, and stomach discomfort associated with hangovers by inhibiting the release of stomach acid and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract. The benefits of consuming Zingiber officinale teas for hangover relief include reducing pain, inflammation, and discomfort, as well as promoting digestion and alleviating nausea. Regular consumption of ginger tea has also been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a potential natural remedy for preventing hangover symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger root. Wash it with water.
Peel the ginger root and slice it into thin pieces.
Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Add the sliced ginger to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it warm to help with hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can help alleviate hangover symptoms, but possible side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and interactions with blood thinning medications. Precautions should be taken when using Zingiber officinale teas, such as starting with small amounts, avoiding excessive consumption, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners like warfarin.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea upon waking up.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale powder per cup of tea.
Consume 2-3 cups throughout the day as needed.
Consider adding honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Repeat as necessary, but not more than 5 cups per day.

preparation_amazon.title: FGO Organic Ginger Tea, 100 Count, Eco-Conscious Tea Bags, Caffeine Free, Packaging May Vary (Pack of 1)

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat hangover symptoms due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba teas help to alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents, such as bilobalide and ginkgolides, work together to improve blood flow, reduce nausea, and alleviate headaches associated with hangovers. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been shown to provide relief from hangover symptoms and improve overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy among those who enjoy social drinking.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Bring the water to a boil in a pot.
Add the Ginkgo biloba leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea and consider adding 1 tablespoon of honey for taste.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat hangover symptoms, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or in combination with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using Ginkgo biloba teas for hangovers, including starting with small doses to assess tolerance, avoiding consumption with other stimulants or medications, and staying hydrated to minimize potential side effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea in the morning after hangover.
Take 1 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba powder in the afternoon.
Repeat Ginkgo biloba tea in the evening after 8 hours.
Continue for 2-3 days, drinking 1 cup of tea twice a day.
Monitor and adjust dosage as needed, up to 2 teaspoons per day.

preparation_amazon.title: Tai Chi Think Sharp Energizing Tea (Ginseng Ginkgo Biloba) 12 Bags

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the licorice root, have been traditionally used to treat hangover ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea associated with hangovers, allowing the body to recover more efficiently. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been found to play a crucial role in mitigating hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been shown to provide several benefits in treating hangover ailments, including reduced severity of symptoms, faster recovery time, and improved overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot or kettle until it boils.
Place the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root in the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea as needed for hangover relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat hangover symptoms, but possible side effects may include sodium retention, leading to swelling in the hands and feet, as well as an increased risk of potassium deficiency. When using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat hangover symptoms, it is recommended to consume it in moderation and to drink plenty of water to help dilute the concentration of glycyrrhizin, as excessive consumption may exacerbate hangover symptoms.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea upon waking up.
Consume 1 tablespoon of licorice root powder with each cup.
Repeat 2-3 cups throughout the day, spaced 1-2 hours apart.
Maintain a balanced diet and stay hydrated with water.
Limit tea consumption to 3-4 days to avoid habituation.

preparation_amazon.title: Pukka Herbal Teas Licorice and Cinnamon - 20 Bags, 20 Count

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat the hangover ailment due to their properties that help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. The quinine content in Cinchona officinalis teas helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, while the flavonoids present in the tea have antioxidant properties that aid in the recovery process. The bioactive constituents, including quinine and flavonoids, work together to help treat the hangover ailment by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting relaxation, and stabilizing blood sugar levels. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat hangover symptoms include relief from nausea and headaches, improved mood, and a faster recovery time.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona bark in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if needed.
Drink the tea slowly to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat hangovers due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use Cinchona officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to consume them in moderation, avoid taking them on an empty stomach, and be cautious when combining them with other medications or substances that may interact with their active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis tea first thing in the morning.
Repeat with 1 cup every 2 hours up to 3 times a day.
Take 2-3 tablespoons of Cinchona officinalis powder with the tea.
Continue for 24-48 hours to alleviate hangover symptoms.
Consult a doctor for proper diagnosis and treatment if necessary.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been used to treat hangover symptoms due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate nausea, headaches, and fatigue. This herbal preparation works by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, thereby alleviating the discomfort associated with a hangover. The bioactive constituents, including valerenic acid, valepotriates, and isovaltrate, in Valeriana officinalis teas contribute to their analgesic and sedative effects, which help to mitigate hangover symptoms. The benefits of using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat hangovers include reducing the severity of symptoms, promoting relaxation, and providing a natural and non-addictive alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Valeriana officinalis roots or 2 teaspoons of dried roots.
Combine the Valeriana officinalis roots with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and pour the tea into a separate cup.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea as needed, ideally 1-2 hours after waking up.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study published in "Praxis", Valeriana officinalis teas for hangover are unlikely to cause impairment, and in fact, may even improve subjective self-assessment, making one feel more alert and active.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can help alleviate hangover symptoms, but consuming large amounts may lead to side effects such as drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. To use Valeriana officinalis teas effectively, it is essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid mixing it with other sedatives or medications to minimize potential interactions and adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Valeriana officinalis tea in the morning after hangover.
Take 1-2 cups of tea every 2-3 hours as needed throughout the day.
Start with small dosages of 1/2 cup and increase as needed.
Combine with hydration and rest for faster recovery.
Consult a doctor if hangover persists or worsens over time.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to alleviate the symptoms of a hangover due to their soothing and calming properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation, calming the digestive system, and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate headaches and stomach discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to their hangover-relieving effects. The benefits of this herbal preparation in treating hangover symptoms include improved sleep quality, reduced nausea and vomiting, and a faster recovery time.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) and set aside.
Steep the dried flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. If using honey, add it to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea and rest for at least 30 minutes to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat hangover symptoms, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, digestive upset, and increased heart rate due to the presence of volatile oils like linalool and linalyl acetate. When using Lavandula angustifolia teas for this purpose, take precautions by drinking it in moderation, avoiding it if you have allergies or sensitivities, and being aware of potential interactions with medications, especially those for anxiety or sleep disorders.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea after waking up.
Take 1-2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia in the tea.
Repeat the tea every 2-3 hours as needed for hangover relief.
Use Lavandula angustifolia tea for 1-2 days after the hangover incident.
Consider consulting a doctor if hangover persists or worsens.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to alleviate hangover symptoms, offering a natural remedy for individuals seeking relief from the adverse effects of excessive alcohol consumption. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, help to treat hangover ailments by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and stabilizing the nervous system. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng, such as ginsenosides and saponins, play a crucial role in mitigating hangover symptoms, including headaches, fatigue, and nausea, by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting overall well-being. By consuming Panax ginseng teas, individuals can experience benefits such as reduced hangover severity, improved mood, and enhanced physical recovery, making it a popular choice among those seeking a natural hangover remedy.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Panax ginseng root.
Add the ginseng root to a cup and pour boiling water over it.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea when it's cool enough, ideally 2 cups a day for hangover relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Fitoterapia", Panax ginseng teas for hangover may be effective in decreasing alcohol consumption and possibly reducing hangover symptoms by reducing alcohol absorption from the gastrointestinal system.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be used to potentially alleviate hangover symptoms, but it may also cause side effects such as insomnia, anxiety, and rapid heartbeat in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming Panax ginseng teas before bedtime or if you are prone to sleep disorders.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Panax ginseng tea in the morning after hangover.
Take 2 tablespoons of Panax ginseng powder with 1 cup of water.
Repeat step 2 every 4-6 hours, for 2-3 days.
Increase the dosage to 3 tablespoons after 2 days, if needed.
Combine with rest, hydration, and healthy food for faster recovery.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms associated with hangover ailments, such as headaches and nausea. The herbal preparation's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the body, reducing the severity of hangover symptoms. The bioactive constituents, including rosmarinic acid and camphor, work together to alleviate headaches, reduce nausea, and promote digestive health, thereby easing hangover discomfort. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas has been reported to help mitigate the negative effects of excessive alcohol consumption and provide relief from hangover symptoms in a natural and non-addictive way.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves, and a tea infuser.
Add the dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the tea infuser.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea slowly and repeat as needed to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to alleviate hangover symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities. When using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat hangover, it is recommended to start with a small amount and gradually increase the dose, as excessive consumption may lead to allergic reactions, interact with medications, and exacerbate stomach issues.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis tea after waking up.
Take 2 tablespoons of dried herb in the tea.
Use 3 times a day for up to 3 days.
Drink at least 8 cups of water throughout the day.
Avoid heavy meals and plan for light, healthy food.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat hangover ailments due to their hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat hangover by supporting the liver's natural detoxification processes, thereby reducing the severity of symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. The bioactive constituents of milk thistle tea, including silymarin and flavonoids, help to protect the liver cells from damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat hangover include reduced inflammation, improved liver function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day, as needed, to help relieve hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat hangover symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use Silybum marianum teas safely, take them in moderation and avoid mixing with other medications or substances, as this may increase the risk of adverse interactions and enhance the side effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea upon waking up.
Consume 1 tablespoon of Silybum marianum extract with breakfast.
Repeat step 2 every 4 hours for 2 days.
Increase dosage to 2 tablespoons if hangover persists.
Continue for 2 more days or as needed for relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas have been traditionally used to treat hangover ailments due to their soothing and calming properties, which help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. The herbal preparation's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, attributed to bioactive constituents like apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress, contributing to its hangover-relieving effects. By promoting relaxation, reducing stress levels, and supporting digestion, Matricaria chamomilla teas provide a natural and non-invasive way to recover from a night of excessive drinking. The benefits of using this herbal preparation to treat hangovers include reduced severity of symptoms, improved mood, and a quicker recovery time, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural remedy.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 2 teaspoons of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot or kettle until it reaches 212°F (100°C).
Place the dried flowers in the tea infuser or strainer and put it in the pot with the hot water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes. Let the mixture sit for a few minutes before straining the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be used to alleviate hangover symptoms, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its sedative properties and potential interactions with other medications. To minimize these side effects, it is essential to consume the tea in moderation and avoid taking it on an empty stomach, as well as to be aware of its potential effects on people with allergies or sensitivities to plants in the Asteraceae family.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Matricaria chamomilla tea in the morning after hangover.
Take 2 cups of tea in the afternoon to ease symptoms.
Consume 1 teaspoon of dried Matricaria chamomilla in tea 3 times a day.
Take Matricaria chamomilla tea for 3 consecutive days to reduce hangover effects.
Replenish fluids by drinking plenty of water while using Matricaria chamomilla tea.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, also known as green tea, have been traditionally used to alleviate hangover symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to treat hangover ailments by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents, including catechins, theaflavins, and thearubigins, play a crucial role in mitigating hangover symptoms by combating free radicals and modulating the gut-brain axis. The benefits of using Camellia sinensis teas to treat hangovers include improved hydration, reduced nausea and headaches, and enhanced mental clarity, making it a popular natural remedy for hangover relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Camellia sinensis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey or sugar to taste.
Drink 1 cup of the tea to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of biological regulators and homeostatic agents", Camellia sinensis teas for hangover may be beneficial due to its high antioxidant capacity, specifically the 50% methanolic extract of Camellia sinensis which showed a significant radical scavenging DPPH assay of 78.95±7.12%, potentially aiding in the mitigation of oxidative stress associated with hangover.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can provide relief from hangover symptoms, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in high amounts or by those with sensitive stomachs. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small amounts, drink plenty of water, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach to help alleviate stomach discomfort.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Camellia sinensis tea as soon as you wake up.
Take 1 tablespoon of Camellia sinensis extract with breakfast.
Consume 1 cup of Camellia sinensis tea every 2 hours throughout the day.
Increase dosage to 2 tablespoons of extract by dinner time.
Continue drinking Camellia sinensis tea for 2-3 days to alleviate hangover symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been used to treat hangover ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and digestive properties. The herbal preparation helps to alleviate hangover symptoms by soothing the stomach, reducing nausea, and replenishing essential nutrients. The bioactive constituents present in Artemisia absinthium teas, including flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and volatile oils, play a crucial role in mitigating hangover symptoms by modulating gut health and reducing inflammation. The benefits of using Artemisia absinthium teas to treat hangover ailments include reduced severity of symptoms, improved digestive health, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea when cool enough to consume and as needed for hangover relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used to treat hangover ailments, but consuming it may result in side effects such as dry mouth, nausea, and dizziness due to its bitter compounds and volatile oils. When using Artemisia absinthium teas for hangover relief, it is essential to take precautions to avoid excessive consumption, as it can lead to allergic reactions, interact with other medications, and exacerbate digestive issues, particularly when consumed in large quantities or by individuals with sensitive stomachs.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, commonly known as peppermint tea, have been used for centuries to help alleviate the symptoms of hangover due to its soothing and digestive properties. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as menthol, menthone, and limonene, which help to calm the stomach, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate headaches and fatigue associated with hangover. By consuming peppermint tea, individuals can experience relief from hangover symptoms, as the menthol and other compounds help to relax the muscles, improve digestion, and promote a sense of well-being. The benefits of using peppermint tea to treat hangover include its natural and non-invasive approach, making it a popular and effective remedy for those seeking a quick and gentle solution.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of water, 2 tablespoons of fresh peppermint leaves, 1 tablespoon of honey
Boil the water in a pot. Let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of fresh peppermint leaves to the cooled water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the peppermint leaves. Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea slowly and enjoy. For hangover relief, drink 2-3 cups of the tea throughout the day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Bulletin of experimental biology and medicine, Mentha x piperita teas for hangover may be beneficial due to the presence of rosmarinic acid, which is one of the marker components found in the sedative tea.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to help alleviate hangover symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals due to its high concentration of menthol and essential oils. To safely use Mentha x piperita teas, it is recommended to start with small amounts, gradually increasing the dose as needed, and to avoid consuming it on an empty stomach to minimize the risk of stomach discomfort.

preparations.herb_desc: Peumus boldus teas, also known as boldo tea, have been traditionally used to help alleviate hangover symptoms due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat hangover ailments by reducing inflammation, soothing the stomach, and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of boldo tea, including boldine and polyphenols, have been found to contribute to its hangover-relieving properties by reducing oxidative stress and modulating the gut-brain axis. Regular consumption of Peumus boldus teas has been reported to provide benefits such as reduced hangover severity, improved digestion, and enhanced liver function, making it a popular natural remedy for hangover relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 2 teaspoons of dried Peumus boldus leaves.
Steep the Peumus boldus leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, to help soothe the stomach.
Drink the Peumus boldus tea slowly and repeat as needed to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Peumus boldus teas may be beneficial for hangovers due to its potent antioxidant properties, which can help scavenge harmful radicals and quench pro-oxidant species in the body.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Peumus boldus teas can be used to treat hangover symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Peumus boldus teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose, avoid consuming it with other stimulants or medications, and be cautious when taking it on an empty stomach to minimize the risk of stomach upset.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been traditionally used to help alleviate hangover symptoms, such as nausea and headaches. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents like sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which help to treat hangovers by promoting liver function, reducing inflammation, and acting as antioxidants. The benefits of using dandelion tea to treat hangovers include its ability to help replenish electrolytes, reduce nausea and vomiting, and provide a natural remedy for dehydration. Furthermore, the anti-inflammatory properties of Taraxacum officinale teas may also contribute to its ability to alleviate hangover symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy among those seeking to recover from excessive alcohol consumption.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Taraxacum officinale leaves, flowers, and roots.
Use 1 tablespoon of the Taraxacum officinale mixture and place it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle and pour it over the Taraxacum officinale mixture.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup using a strainer or the tea infuser.
Drink the tea slowly, ideally 1-2 cups, to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used as a potential remedy for hangover symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its diuretic properties and potential interactions with other medications. To minimize the risk of side effects, it is recommended to start with small amounts, drink plenty of water, and avoid using Taraxacum officinale teas if you have stomach problems or are sensitive to diuretics.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to alleviate hangover symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat hangovers by reducing inflammation, alleviating nausea and headaches, and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, glycoproteins, and phenolic acids, are responsible for its medicinal effects, which help to mitigate the negative effects of excessive alcohol consumption. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat hangovers include reduced severity and duration of symptoms, improved hydration, and enhanced antioxidant defenses.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a heat-resistant cup.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, up to 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Plant foods for human nutrition (Dordrecht, Netherlands), Echinacea purpurea teas for hangover are not specifically mentioned or studied in the provided review.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to alleviate hangover symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and headaches in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, and be cautious if you have allergies or are sensitive to any of the ingredients.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have gained popularity as a natural remedy to alleviate the symptoms of hangovers due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel-like substance extracted from the leaves of the aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to replenish the body's stores and counteract the effects of dehydration and oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption. The bioactive constituents of aloe barbadensis, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce hangover symptoms such as headache, nausea, and fatigue. By consuming aloe barbadensis teas, individuals can potentially reduce their hangover symptoms and enjoy a faster recovery, making it a valuable natural remedy for those seeking a holistic approach to mitigating the effects of excessive drinking.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of aloe barbadensis gel, and a tea infuser.
Cut the aloe leaf and scoop out the gel, then add it to the tea infuser.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer.
Steep the aloe gel in the water for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Strain the liquid and drink it warm or let it cool to drink as a hangover remedy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat hangover symptoms, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea due to its strong laxative properties. To use Aloe barbadensis teas safely, take it in moderation, start with small doses, and avoid consuming it on an empty stomach to minimize potential side effects like dehydration and electrolyte imbalances.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas have been traditionally used to treat hangover symptoms due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate headaches, nausea, and stomach discomfort. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and alkaloids, such as harmine and harmaline, have been found to have a sedative effect, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, while also exhibiting antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. The tea's ability to calm the nervous system and promote a sense of well-being makes it a popular natural remedy for hangover relief, allowing individuals to recover more quickly and comfortably. By consuming Passiflora incarnata tea, individuals can experience the benefits of reduced hangover symptoms, improved mood, and a faster return to normal bodily functions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Passiflora incarnata flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flowers to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate cup.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Central nervous system agents in medicinal chemistry, Passiflora incarnata teas for hangover may have potential, but there is no direct information about this specific use in the provided study, which only discusses its anxiolytic effects and sedative potential.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be used to treat the hangover ailment, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, such as starting with a small dose, being cautious when operating heavy machinery or driving, and avoiding use if you have a history of liver or kidney disease.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to alleviate the symptoms of a hangover, and its properties make it a promising natural remedy for this ailment. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Curcuma longa teas help to reduce the severity of hangover symptoms, such as nausea and headaches. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its hangover-relieving properties. By consuming Curcuma longa teas, individuals can experience a range of benefits, including reduced hangover severity, improved digestion, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Turmeric root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Add the Turmeric root to the tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional).
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, there is no specific information about the effects of Curcuma longa teas for hangovers, but it mentions that Curcuma longa contains curcumin as a phytochemical compound which has potential to enhance the digestive system and alleviate pain and discomfort in patients.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be used to alleviate hangover symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea, especially when consumed in large quantities or combined with other medications. To use Curcuma longa teas effectively, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to drink plenty of water throughout the day to minimize stomach discomfort and potential interactions with other substances.

preparations.herb_desc: Tanacetum parthenium teas, also known as feverfew teas, have been used to treat hangover symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate headaches and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents present in feverfew, such as parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones, contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential remedy for hangover relief. By consuming feverfew teas, individuals may experience a reduction in nausea and vomiting, as well as a decrease in the severity of headaches and fatigue associated with hangovers. Overall, Tanacetum parthenium teas offer a natural and herbal solution for alleviating hangover symptoms, providing relief without the adverse effects of conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Tanacetum parthenium flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the flowers.
Add honey to taste, if needed, to make the tea sweeter.
Drink the tea when you need relief from hangover symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Tanacetum parthenium teas can be used to treat hangover symptoms, but possible side effects include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its feverfew properties. To minimize potential issues, individuals should use Tanacetum parthenium teas in moderation and avoid taking it if they have allergies, are pregnant, or breastfeeding, as it may cause uterine contractions and affect fetal development.
